message:
fix(ipc,durable-state,watchdog): harden input validation and default-to-write IPC scope

Three hardening improvements to prevent misclassification and edge-case panics:

1. IPC scope classification (ipc.rs):
   - Invert the default: unknown RPC commands now require Write scope instead of Read,
     following the principle of least privilege. A new command that isn't yet classified
     should be treated as potentially mutating rather than assumed safe.
   - Expand the explicit Read whitelist: help, quickstart, state, get-text, wait-for,
     search, cass, why, rules, agents, health, and read-only subcommands of events,
     workflow, accounts, reservations, mission, and tx.
   - Normalize command/subcommand strings with trim + to_ascii_lowercase for
     case-insensitive matching.

2. Durable state retention (durable_state.rs):
   - Extract normalize_max_checkpoints(max) that clamps to minimum 1, preventing
     a max_checkpoints=0 from draining all checkpoints on every save.
   - Extract enforce_retention_limit() helper shared between checkpoint() and
     rollback() — rollback previously didn't enforce the limit.
   - Replace .unwrap() with .expect("checkpoint must exist") for clarity.

3. Watchdog config sanitization (semantic_anomaly_watchdog.rs):
   - Add sanitize_watchdog_config() that clamps queue_capacity and batch_size to
     minimum 1 (preventing zero-capacity ArrayQueue panics) and ensures
     max_segment_bytes >= min_segment_bytes.
   - Apply sanitization at SemanticAnomalyWatchdog::start() entry point.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
